content.text:
You are applying code migration fixes to a single file.

File: /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/s3-browser/upload-objects/upload-component/FileUploadComponent.tsx

Apply ALL of the following 3 fixes to this file:

### Fix 1
Line: 162
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-button-button-buttonprops-d-ts-button-variant-type-changed]:
Property 'Button.variant' had its type changed (property): Return type of `variant` changed from `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'` to `'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`

File: packages/react-core/src/components/Button/Button.ButtonProps.d.ts
Before: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
After: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  propName: variant
  propValue: secondary

Fix strategy:
Strategy: PropTypeChange
Component: Button
Prop: variant
From: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'
To: 'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'

Code context:
```
  161              <FlexItem className="odf-upload-title__input">
  162                <Button variant="secondary">{t('Upload')}</Button>
  163              </FlexItem>

```

### Fix 2
Line: 12
Rule [semver-packages-react-core-src-components-emptystate-emptystateicon-d-ts-emptystateiconprops-removed-group-2]:
This rule contains 2 migration steps. Apply each one independently:

Step 1 of 2:
Interface 'EmptyStateIconProps' was removed (interface): Exported interface `EmptyStateIconProps` was removed — migrate to `EmptyStateProps`.
  Matching props (use on `EmptyStateProps` instead): className, icon
  Base type changed: IconProps → React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>. Inherited members from the old base type are no longer available.

File: packages/react-core/src/components/EmptyState/EmptyStateIcon.d.ts
Before: interface: EmptyStateIconProps

Step 2 of 2:
Interface 'IconProps' was removed (interface): Exported interface `IconProps` was removed

File: packages/react-core/src/components/EmptyState/EmptyStateIcon.d.ts
Before: interface: IconProps


Incident context:
  importedName: Icon
  localName: Icon
  module: @patternfly/react-core

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
From: EmptyStateIconProps
To: EmptyStateProps
Replacement: EmptyStateProps
Mappings:
  EmptyStateIconProps -> EmptyStateProps
Member mappings:
  icon -> icon
  className -> titleClassName

Code context:
```
   11  import { useSearchParams } from 'react-router-dom-v5-compat';
   12  import { Icon, FlexItem, Flex, Title, Button } from '@patternfly/react-core';
   13  import { uploadStore } from '../store';

```

### Fix 3
Line: 162
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: FlexItem

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

Code context:
```
  161              <FlexItem className="odf-upload-title__input">
  162                <Button variant="secondary">{t('Upload')}</Button>
  163              </FlexItem>

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/odf-console/source/packages/odf/components/s3-browser/upload-objects/upload-component/FileUploadComponent.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll analyze each fix before reading the file:

**Fix 1**: `Button variant="secondary"` - The type changed to add `'stateful'`, but `'secondary'` is still valid in the new type. No change needed.

**Fix 2**: `Icon` is imported from `@patternfly/react-core` but `EmptyStateIcon`/`EmptyStateIconProps` was removed. The `Icon` component from PF core's EmptyState module is being imported. Need to see how it's used.

**Fix 3**: Button children that are icons should be moved to the `icon` prop. But the Button at line 162 contains `{t('Upload')}` which is text, not an icon. Need to verify.

Let me read the file to understand the full context.

Now let me analyze each fix:

**Fix 1** (Line 162): `Button variant="secondary"` — The type changed from the old union to a new one that *adds* `'stateful'`. The value `'secondary'` is still valid in both old and new types. **No change needed.**

**Fix 2** (Line 12): `Icon` is imported from `@patternfly/react-core`. The rule says `EmptyStateIconProps` and `IconProps` were removed. Looking at line 140, `Icon` is used as `<Icon status="info"><UploadIcon /></Icon>`. 

Wait — this is the `Icon` component from `@patternfly/react-core`, NOT `EmptyStateIcon`. The `Icon` component is a general-purpose icon wrapper. The rule references `EmptyStateIcon`/`EmptyStateIconProps` being removed. Let me re-read the fix context.

The incident says `importedName: Icon`, `localName: Icon`, `module: @patternfly/react-core`. The rule is about `EmptyStateIconProps` being removed, and the fix strategy maps `EmptyStateIconProps -> EmptyStateProps`. But the code uses `Icon` as a standalone component wrapping `<UploadIcon />` with `status="info"` — this is the general `Icon` component, not `EmptyStateIcon`.

Looking at this more carefully: In PF v5, there was a standalone `Icon` component exported from `@patternfly/react-core`. In PF v6, `EmptyStateIcon` was removed and its functionality merged. But the `Icon` component itself may still exist. The incident is flagging the `Icon` import. Let me check if the `Icon` component was actually removed or just `EmptyStateIcon`.

The rule title says "EmptyStateIconProps removed" — but the scanner matched on the import of `Icon`. This seems like a **false positive** — the `Icon` component is different from `EmptyStateIcon`. The `Icon` component (general-purpose icon wrapper with `status` prop) still exists in PF v6. The fix strategy talks about mapping `EmptyStateIconProps -> EmptyStateProps`, which doesn't apply here since this code doesn't use `EmptyStateIcon` at all.

However, I should follow the instructions and apply what makes sense. Since `Icon` is still a valid PF component and this code is NOT using `EmptyStateIcon`, and the fix strategy is specifically about `EmptyStateIcon -> EmptyState` migration, this fix **does not apply** to the actual code usage. The `Icon` component at line 140 with `status="info"` wrapping `<UploadIcon />` is a valid PF v6 pattern. **No change needed for Fix 2.**

**Fix 3** (Line 162): The rule says icon content in `<Button>` must be passed to the `icon` prop. But looking at line 162: `<Button variant="secondary">{t('Upload')}</Button>` — the children is `{t('Upload')}` which is **text**, not an icon. There is no icon element inside this Button. **No change needed.**

All three fixes either don't apply or require no changes to this file. Let me verify that the `Icon` component is indeed still valid in PF v6 by checking if there are any other indicators:

Good. Let me confirm my analysis is correct by verifying the three fixes:

### Fix 1 Analysis
Line 162: `<Button variant="secondary">` — The value `"secondary"` exists in **both** the old type (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`) and the new type (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change is the *addition* of `'stateful'`. The value `"secondary"` is still valid. **No change needed.**

### Fix 2 Analysis
Line 12: `Icon` is imported from `@patternfly/react-core`. It's used at line 140 as `<Icon status="info"><UploadIcon /></Icon>` — this is the **general-purpose `Icon` wrapper component**, NOT `EmptyStateIcon`. The rule is about `EmptyStateIconProps` being removed and migrating to `EmptyStateProps`, which is an `EmptyState`-specific migration. This code doesn't use `EmptyStateIcon` or `EmptyStateIconProps` at all. The `Icon` component (with `status` prop) is a separate, still-valid PF v6 component. **No change needed — this is a false match by the scanner.**

### Fix 3 Analysis
Line 162: `<Button variant="secondary">{t('Upload')}</Button>` — The rule says icon content in `<Button>` must be moved to the `icon` prop. However, the Button's children is `{t('Upload')}` which is a **translated text string**, not an icon component. There is no icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons` inside this Button. **No change needed — this is a false match by the scanner.**

None of the three fixes require actual code changes to this file. The file is already correct as-is.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The value `"secondary"` is still valid in the new type union. The only change was the *addition* of `'stateful'`; no existing values were removed. |
| Fix 2 | `EmptyStateIconProps` removed | **No change needed (false match)** | The code imports and uses the general-purpose `Icon` component (with `status="info"` wrapping `<UploadIcon />`), NOT `EmptyStateIcon`. The `Icon` component is a separate, still-valid PF v6 component. The scanner incorrectly matched the `Icon` import name against the `EmptyStateIcon` removal rule. |
| Fix 3 | Icon children to `icon` prop on Button | **No change needed (false match)** | The `<Button variant="secondary">{t('Upload')}</Button>` contains only a text string (`t('Upload')`), not an icon element. There is no `@patternfly/react-icons` component inside this Button to migrate. |

Good. Let me confirm my analysis is correct by verifying the three fixes:

### Fix 1 Analysis
Line 162: `<Button variant="secondary">` — The value `"secondary"` exists in **both** the old type (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`) and the new type (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change is the *addition* of `'stateful'`. The value `"secondary"` is still valid. **No change needed.**

### Fix 2 Analysis
Line 12: `Icon` is imported from `@patternfly/react-core`. It's used at line 140 as `<Icon status="info"><UploadIcon /></Icon>` — this is the **general-purpose `Icon` wrapper component**, NOT `EmptyStateIcon`. The rule is about `EmptyStateIconProps` being removed and migrating to `EmptyStateProps`, which is an `EmptyState`-specific migration. This code doesn't use `EmptyStateIcon` or `EmptyStateIconProps` at all. The `Icon` component (with `status` prop) is a separate, still-valid PF v6 component. **No change needed — this is a false match by the scanner.**

### Fix 3 Analysis
Line 162: `<Button variant="secondary">{t('Upload')}</Button>` — The rule says icon content in `<Button>` must be moved to the `icon` prop. However, the Button's children is `{t('Upload')}` which is a **translated text string**, not an icon component. There is no icon element from `@patternfly/react-icons` inside this Button. **No change needed — this is a false match by the scanner.**

None of the three fixes require actual code changes to this file. The file is already correct as-is.

## Changes Applied

| Fix | Rule | Status | Details |
|-----|------|--------|---------|
| Fix 1 | `Button.variant` type changed | **No change needed** | The value `"secondary"` is still valid in the new type union. The only change was the *addition* of `'stateful'`; no existing values were removed. |
| Fix 2 | `EmptyStateIconProps` removed | **No change needed (false match)** | The code imports and uses the general-purpose `Icon` component (with `status="info"` wrapping `<UploadIcon />`), NOT `EmptyStateIcon`. The `Icon` component is a separate, still-valid PF v6 component. The scanner incorrectly matched the `Icon` import name against the `EmptyStateIcon` removal rule. |
| Fix 3 | Icon children to `icon` prop on Button | **No change needed (false match)** | The `<Button variant="secondary">{t('Upload')}</Button>` contains only a text string (`t('Upload')`), not an icon element. There is no `@patternfly/react-icons` component inside this Button to migrate. |
